hero.proofHeadline: 4+ years shipping production software. 7+ years in engineering background.
hero.proofSubheadline: Now focused on AI product development

hero.ctaPrimaryHint: See how I design and ship real systems
hero.ctaSecondaryHint: Open for hiring, partnerships, and founder conversations

about.label: About Me
about.title: From Blueprints to Bytecode to Intelligence
about.subtitle: A systems engineer who found his most resilient medium
about.act1Body:
The first chapter of my career was spent designing MEP systems for commercial developments at Toparch in Bangkok. MEP engineering is about managing constraints at scale — budget, physics, building codes, and the needs of thousands of people who will inhabit a system you design but never see.

That is where I learned what good engineering actually is: a solution that survives first contact with reality.

I was capable, stable, and yet — I wrote my first line of JavaScript on evenings and weekends and realized software was the same discipline in a different, more fluid medium.
about.act2Body:
In April 2021, I resigned.

No job waiting, no bootcamp, just a year of savings, conviction, and the systems-thinking discipline built in physical engineering.

I taught myself JavaScript, TypeScript, React, and the fundamentals of modern software architecture. I scored 890 on my first TOEIC — opening doors to international opportunities many Thai engineers miss. Twelve months later, I had a portfolio and walked into MAQE.

The bet paid off.
about.act3Body:
Three years at MAQE Bangkok, one of Thailand's premier digital agencies, I shipped software that real people use — home-buying platforms for thousands of AP Thailand customers, decentralized governance tools for Web3 communities, and AI event platforms that put intelligence into creation.

I also learned what matters more than any tech stack: a value-compounding engineer is one who makes the team better, not just the codebase. I co-founded the FEDx Frontend Guild, hosted knowledge sessions, mentored peers, and became the engineer clients specifically requested for.
about.act4Body:
I joined TeamStack in 2025 — a product-focused environment where I could work on a product, not just deliver client projects. Currently building The Air Product: an AI-first internal tool with a 3-year strategic roadmap, working directly with the CEO on vision and architecture.

This is the point where I am closest to the work I want to do ultimately: building products that solve real problems with teams that care about outcomes, in markets that need them.

The ten-year goal is SaaS products in Southeast Asia. Every project, every certification, and every deliberate technology choice is preparation. The Gaia Project — a personal second brain and life operating system — is what happens when a systems engineer turns the lens inward.
about.personalityBody:
MBTI: INTJ — The Architect. I design systems before I build them, and I prefer precise vision over broad ambiguity. I am direct, autonomous, and allergic to work that doesn't compound.

Professionally, I believe in three things: relentless improvement, autonomy earned through integrity, and leverage through knowledge — learning things that actually make me and everyone around me better, not just checking boxes.

contact.label: Contact
contact.title: Start the Right Conversation
contact.subtitle: Whether it's AI engineering, product ownership, collaboration, or just a hello — choose the path that gets us there faster.
contact.formIntentLabel: What would you like to discuss?
contact.successMessage: Thanks for reaching out. I'll get back to you within 24 hours.
contact.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again or email me directly.

hire-ai.label: Hire as AI Engineer
hire-ai.heading: Let's talk AI engineering
hire-ai.preview: Tell me about the AI product you're building. What's the problem it solves, what's the technical context, and what gap you're trying to fill. I'll reply with genuine thoughts on fit.
hire-ai.placeholder: E.g. We're building a RAG-based internal knowledge tool and need someone who can own the AI integration layer and frontend...

hire-po.label: Hire as Product Owner
hire-po.heading: Let's talk product ownership
hire-po.preview: Tell me about your product and team. What decisions need to be owned, what's the technical complexity, and what success looks like at 6 months. Engineers who think in products are rare — I'm one.
hire-po.placeholder: E.g. We're a Series A startup with a technical product and need a PO who can translate between engineering and business...

collaborate.label: Co-build Something
collaborate.heading: Let's build together
collaborate.preview: Have an idea for a SaaS product — especially targeting Southeast Asia? Tell me the problem, the market, and why now. I'm actively looking for the right co-founder conversation.
collaborate.placeholder: E.g. I've been working on an AI tool for Thai SMEs and need a technical co-founder who can own the product...

general.label: Just Saying Hi
general.heading: Say hi
general.preview: Even if you're not sure of fit yet, send context and I'll reply thoughtfully. I usually respond within 24 hours on weekdays.

timeline.label: Experience
timeline.title: A 7+ Year Engineering Arc
timeline.subtitle: From MEP systems to self-taught software to AI-first product building — with proof at every stage
timeline.visionDescription: I’m now optimizing for AI-first product roles where systems thinking, ownership, and founder proximity matter — and where this trajectory keeps compounding.

chapter-foundation.title: The Foundation
chapter-foundation.description: MEP engineering built the systems-thinking baseline that still shapes how complex software gets designed today.

chapter-bet.title: The Bet
chapter-bet.description: A deliberate reset: leaving stability, self-studying software full-time, and proving a new career could be built from first principles.

chapter-craft.title: The Craft
chapter-craft.description: The MAQE chapter where software delivery became professional craft, client trust, and repeatable execution.

chapter-frontier.title: The Frontier
chapter-frontier.description: Web3 systems, internal leadership, and deeper ownership showed up at the same time.

chapter-intelligence.title: The Intelligence Layer
chapter-intelligence.description: The point where AI stopped being interesting theory and became production responsibility.

chapter-vision.title: The Vision
chapter-vision.description: TeamStack turns client-delivery experience into product ownership, founder proximity, and AI-first product building.

chapter-acceleration.title: The Acceleration
chapter-acceleration.description: 2026 marks the move from AI experiments to deep AI-human pairing in every layer of delivery.

toparch-mep.title: MEP Engineer — Toparch
toparch-mep.summary: First professional role as an MEP engineer in Bangkok. Designed building systems, read blueprints, and debugged real-world infrastructure constraints.
toparch-mep.description: Spent 2.3 years designing mechanical, electrical, and plumbing systems for commercial buildings. Every project was a constraint-satisfaction problem involving budget, physics, regulations, and the needs of people who would live inside these systems.
toparch-mep.impact: Built the systems-thinking baseline that now shapes how complex software gets designed, debugged, and maintained.

career-pivot.title: Resigned. Started Over. On Purpose.
career-pivot.summary: Left a stable engineering career to self-study programming full-time. No job lined up, no bootcamp, just a laptop and conviction.
career-pivot.description: Spent a year rebuilding from scratch: JavaScript, HTML, CSS, software architecture fundamentals, and portfolio projects. The pivot was financially risky and emotionally expensive, but it proved a new career could be built deliberately.
career-pivot.impact: Established the evidence that I can rebuild competence in a new domain without external structure.

toeic-first.title: TOEIC 890 — First Attempt
toeic-first.summary: Scored 890/990 while simultaneously learning software engineering — an intentional investment in international readiness.
toeic-first.description: English fluency was part of the plan, not a side goal. I treated communication as a professional leverage point in the same way I treated coding practice.
toeic-first.impact: Removed a major barrier to global-facing work and cross-border collaboration.

join-maqe.title: Joined MAQE Bangkok
join-maqe.summary: Started as a professional Software Engineer after a 12-month self-study pivot and entered a high-trust agency environment serving enterprise clients.
join-maqe.description: Joined one of Bangkok’s premier digital agencies and began shipping software for Japanese enterprise clients and global brands. This was the proving ground where the pivot became a profession.
join-maqe.impact: Confirmed that systems thinking from MEP engineering compounds quickly in software delivery.

ap-thai.title: AP Thai — Property Discovery Platform
ap-thai.summary: Consumer-facing home discovery platform for one of Thailand’s largest property developers. Early proof of scale, analytics, and production quality.
ap-thai.description: Built a modernized property-discovery experience with CMS-backed architecture, analytics instrumentation, and mobile-first delivery. It was one of the first times I saw user-behavior data directly shape what got built next.
ap-thai.impact: Shipped a homebuying journey for thousands of users and learned how analytics changes product decision-making.

peatix-series.title: Peatix Series — Long-Term Product Partnership
peatix-series.summary: A long-running engagement that evolved into the deepest client-trust proof in the portfolio. Became the trusted frontend owner over multiple years.
peatix-series.description: Over a multi-year client relationship, I moved from contributor to trusted owner. The work compounded across product delivery, technical judgment, and cross-cultural communication.
peatix-series.impact: A direct client called me a primary reason they wanted to keep working with MAQE.

fedx-guild.title: FEDx Guild — Knowledge Leadership
fedx-guild.summary: Co-founded and led the Frontend Excellence Guild, turning personal craft into team-wide leverage over 400+ days.
fedx-guild.description: Organized sessions, aligned standards, and became a knowledge anchor across teams. The guild became evidence that value compounds beyond the codebase when you help others level up too.
fedx-guild.impact: Praew described me as someone who “glued everyone together” and sits at the top rank of trust.

token-gating.title: Token Gating — NFT Access Control
token-gating.summary: Full-stack NFT access control shipped under delivery pressure. Learned Web3 by building it in production, not by studying it in isolation.
token-gating.description: Built a reusable access-control system validating token ownership across chains, with frontend, API, and infrastructure all moving together.
token-gating.impact: Proved that unfamiliar domains can become shippable systems quickly when approached through systems thinking.

maqe-website-v5.title: MAQE Website v5 — Full Ownership
maqe-website-v5.summary: Primary owner for MAQE’s corporate website rebuild across architecture, design implementation, performance, and maintenance.
maqe-website-v5.description: The first sustained end-to-end ownership experience where I cared for the whole system, not just the next ticket. It sharpened both product judgment and collaboration with design.
maqe-website-v5.impact: A design collaborator explicitly highlighted my systematic thinking as a rare trait.

toeic-second.title: TOEIC 910 — Second Attempt
toeic-second.summary: Re-certified with a higher score to maintain international standards and demonstrate continuous improvement in global communication.
toeic-second.description: Achieving 910/990 was about maintaining an edge. High-stakes communication with international stakeholders and founders requires zero friction, and this was the proof of that readiness.
toeic-second.impact: Reinforced confidence for high-stakes international stakeholder management and product leadership.

online-catalog.title: B2B Online Catalog Platform
online-catalog.summary: Headless B2B commerce work that replaced manual sales workflows with a digital catalog and business-ready frontend experience.
online-catalog.description: A deep introduction to GraphQL architecture, Magento, and the very different UX constraints of B2B workflows: bulk ordering, pricing logic, and operational context.
online-catalog.impact: Expanded range beyond B2C and taught me how business context changes technical choices.

tangier-dao.title: Tangier — Web3 DAO Giving Circles
tangier-dao.summary: Decentralized governance infrastructure on Ethereum with smart contracts, proposal flows, voting, and fund distribution.
tangier-dao.description: Built the frontend and wrote the Solidity contracts that governed community proposals and treasury logic. It is a rare production Solidity proof point in the local market.
tangier-dao.impact: Demonstrated frontier-tech range while still shipping complete, user-facing systems.

ai-event-platform.title: AI Event Creation Platform — Production GenAI
ai-event-platform.summary: First production AI feature shipped to real users, where the job stopped being “build interfaces” and became “build interfaces that think.”
ai-event-platform.description: Integrated generative AI into a live event-management product to help real organizers create better content faster. The challenge was not demo quality but production reliability and UX fit.
ai-event-platform.impact: Established reusable AI integration patterns and proved I can bridge the gap between AI demos and AI products.

aws-certified.title: AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner
aws-certified.summary: Formalized cloud architecture knowledge built across years of production delivery during the TeamStack transition.
aws-certified.description: Certification mattered less as a badge than as confirmation that years of infrastructure exposure had become coherent architectural judgment.
aws-certified.impact: Improved confidence in infrastructure decisions and systems-level discussions.

join-teamstack.title: Transition to TeamStack — Building From the Ground Up
join-teamstack.summary: Moved from client delivery into a product-focused environment with founder proximity, direct ownership, and a 3-year product roadmap.
join-teamstack.description: This shift mattered because it changed the context: not shipping for a client, but helping shape a product from the inside with direct strategic collaboration.
join-teamstack.impact: Created the closest environment yet to the kind of product building I want to do long term.

the-air-product.title: The Air Product — AI-First SaaS
the-air-product.summary: Current flagship work: building an AI-first internal product with a 3-year strategic vision, directly with the CEO across product, architecture, and UX.
the-air-product.description: This is the strongest founder-proximity proof in the portfolio. The work is active, strategic, and deeply product-shaped rather than task-shaped.
the-air-product.impact: Active project. CEO feedback directly validates reliability, initiative, and contribution beyond day-to-day delivery.

teamstack-roster.title: TeamStack Roster — Team Management Product
teamstack-roster.summary: Shipped a real team-management product to MVP with end-to-end ownership from architecture to release.
teamstack-roster.description: One of the clearest 0→1 product-delivery proofs in the portfolio. The product demanded product judgment, execution discipline, and cross-functional alignment.
teamstack-roster.impact: First product I can point to as mine across both internal architecture and user-facing delivery.

gaia-project.title: Gaia Project — Personal Life Operating System
gaia-project.summary: A personal second brain and life operating system that applies systems thinking inward — tracking work, learning, goals, and reflection.
gaia-project.description: Gaia is the founder-trajectory signal expressed personally: a systems engineer designing leverage not just in products, but in life itself.
gaia-project.impact: Acts as the personal infrastructure behind how I learn, document, and think long term.

portfolio-refactor.title: Portfolio Localization & Refactor
portfolio-refactor.summary: Full-scale refactor of this portfolio using AI-pairing to implement next-intl, centralized content management, and SEO optimization.
portfolio-refactor.description: Implemented deep localization (EN/TH) across the entire stack. This wasn't just a translation task, but a structural refactor of how content is managed and served, ensuring the site is production-ready and globally accessible.
portfolio-refactor.impact: Achieved a 100% centralized content model and proved the efficiency of AI-human pair programming for complex structural refactors.

footer.headline: Software Engineer by Profession, Lifelong Learner at Heart

testimonials.label: Testimonials
testimonials.title: Voices from the Field
testimonials.subtitle: Real words from clients, managers, and collaborators who have seen my work in the arena.

andreas.summaryQuote: Your main goal at TeamStack is to provide value for PTX—and you have done so in spades.
andreas.fullQuote: Your main goal at TeamStack is to provide value for PTX—and you have done so in spades. Proof is in the contract extension and client feedback. I appreciate greatly your can-do attitude. Whatever I throw at you, you take in stride.

edward.summaryQuote: First is a great asset to our project and also a primary reason why I want to keep working with MAQE.
edward.fullQuote: For our project, First is responsible for all of the frontend development work. During practically every sprint, First has demonstrated all of these things - providing guidance, removing obstacles, collaborating effectively, sharing knowledge, and contributing to the success of the team and project. I believe First has done a tremendous job of taking ownership and responsibility and we trust his knowledge and expertise. First is a great asset to our project and also a primary reason why I want to keep working with MAQE. He truly exemplifies characteristics that I value - thoughtful, considerate, kind, professional, responsible, and the list goes on. First is not only a great engineer, but a truly wonderful person. We greatly appreciate the opportunity to work with First.

daniel.summaryQuote: He also has set a very high standard for himself for the work he is doing - which makes Ed and myself feel very secure when First is involved in a feature, as we know we can rely on him to make something great.
daniel.fullQuote: First has played such an integral part in the success of our team's ability to deploy new features. In general, I appreciate how committed First is to being there for everyone involved in this development process. He has a strong openness to using every experience and new challenge as an important opportunity for learning something new - and then sharing what he has learned with the rest of us. He also has set a very high standard for himself for the work he is doing - which makes Ed and myself feel very secure when First is involved in a feature, as we know we can rely on him to make something great. He is very open to collaboration and he has worked with Bank to clear many obstacles - especially by introducing a detailed overview of how he is solving bugs and QA related items, outlining what went wrong and how it was fixed. It is very valuable information he is sharing with everyone. First's motivation and dedication is a really valuable component to team success and team morale with this project.

pai.summaryQuote: Well organized - First always find a method for the team to work smoothly and easily sync.
pai.fullQuote:
Well organized - First always finds a method for the team to work smoothly and easily sync. For example, he introduced tools like Excalidraw to create flowcharts (e.g., login, checkout). We can use those flowcharts for reference and to communicate with the Peatix team. When we encounter complex requirements such as ticket statuses, he always lists down all the cases and collects them in a Google Sheet to make sure everyone is on the same page.

Great communication - I think First has the best English skills in our Dev team, so he's always the person who helps the team communicate with the Peatix team and helps clarify when both parties are not aligned due to language barriers. You're the MVP for this. 😆

Sharing and seeking new knowledge - First is always involved in the FE guild to share his knowledge and even leads the FedX guild. It's not easy to multitask like that, but he always nails it. 👍

I think First has shown great signs of being a good developer, and I can even see signs of him being a good PM (if you're interested, LOL). So just keep up the good work ka. The more things you experience, the stronger you become 💪

meow.summaryQuote: Think systematically. This trait of yours is a rare gem to encounter. It applies to your working process, problem-solving approach, and how to work better as a team.
meow.fullQuote:
I often work with First on MAQE Website as a designer. Here's what I admire about you:

• Reliable & Responsible: It's admirable that you usually share your working progress and thoughts behind those solutions every week, not only completed tasks. You can manage your tasks in sync with team members like Bank, Big, and me. I'm grateful to have your goodwill and skills in the team.

• Think systematically: This trait of yours is a rare gem to encounter. It applies to your working process, problem-solving approach, and how to work better as a team. You take one step further to solve the problem in the long run (e.g. the recent issue with padding).

• Open-minded/Compromisable: When we discussed the technical feasibility of particular designs, you shared opinions and built on top of each other ideas to find better ways to reach the same goal.

praew.summaryQuote: My top choice for initiating impactful projects
praew.fullQuote:
He is incredibly supportive; He listens well and is open-minded - I feel very comfortable expressing my opinions freely with him.

My top choice for initiating impactful projects; He is eager to explore. His enthusiasm for collaboration and learning new things is evident, making it inspiring and positive to work with him.

Demonstrates resilience and adaptability; He adeptly handles unexpected incidents and seamlessly adapts to workflow changes, facilitating a smooth collaboration.

big.summaryQuote: Your eagerness and numerous contributions create a ripple that causes a positive, learning environment for the team.
big.fullQuote:
There are so many good things from you! You help on various projects apart from the billable (Peatix) project: the MAQE Website, linting packages, guild contributions and initiations (notably FE & FEDX), and those sessions outside of working hours!

Your eagerness to learn and positive energy create a positive vibe for the people around you. There are many traits that are very good to mention: your presentation ability, and your attention to detail in POCs and research.

Your eagerness and numerous contributions create a ripple that causes a positive, learning environment for the team. I can say, without a doubt, that First is a good developer.

bank.summaryQuote: I'm sure that whenever someone encounters an issue, First will always step up, address it, and quickly get to the root of the problem.
bank.fullQuote:
I'm sure that whenever someone encounters an issue, First will always step up, address it, and quickly get to the root of the problem. He always lets me know if he has questions or if I've missed something. It's great that he asks when he's unsure and initiates conversations with the team for discussion.

Sometimes, when the frontend needs support from the backend, he understands the limitations of the customer's system, which may restrict my ability to implement his specific solution. Then, we find another solution together.

Using tools like Excalidraw to communicate with the customer is great. I like that he always ensures we're seeing things from the same perspective.

It's not only about work, either; he often introduces good things, like Duolingo, to the team! :D

chok.summaryQuote: He always raises his hand to contribute to extra work that benefits the company, such as the MAQE website, developer standards, and linting.
chok.fullQuote:
He always raises his hand to contribute to extra work that benefits the company, such as the MAQE website, standards and linting for developers, and guild contributions. Even though he takes on so much, he still maintains high performance on his main project.

Another thing—not just over the past six months, but consistently—is that he likes helping those around him improve their soft skills, such as English and financial tracking. I have also benefited from this; my English skills improved after I practiced with him.

ning.summaryQuote: His communication style is engaging and sincere, which helps build trust and align both team and customer.
ning.fullQuote:
First is proactive in planning and drive team actions. He has a strong organization and structured work. His communication style is engaging and sincere, which helps build trust and align both team and customer. First's positive attitude towards learning is also inspiring for the team.

You are a strong team player with excellent communication and leadership skills. You work independently, provide valuable feedback, and encourage teamwork. Your ability to align the team and share knowledge is greatly appreciated.

production-ai.description: Integrated OpenAI SDK into a live event platform used by real organizers — not a demo, not a side project. I know the difference between AI that works in a notebook and AI that survives production.
production-ai.proof: AI Event Platform + The Air Product at TeamStack

product-thinking.title: Thinks Product, Not Tickets
product-thinking.description: I host guild sessions, write internal docs, and built a personal second brain to manage my own learning system. Engineers who think about product impact ship features that actually matter to the business.
product-thinking.proof: FEDx Guild (434 days) + MAQE Website v5 ownership

full-stack-delivery.title: Owns the Full Delivery Chain
full-stack-delivery.description: From Figma handoff to AWS deployment. No waiting for specialists, no throwing over the fence. From MEP blueprints to Solidity contracts — I've always operated across the full system.
full-stack-delivery.proof: 7 years across MEP → Frontend → Full-stack → AI

fast-domain-mastery.title: Masters New Domains Under Pressure
fast-domain-mastery.description: MEP Engineer → self-taught dev → Web3 → AI. Every domain switch delivered production software. I don't spend months studying before contributing — I learn by shipping.
fast-domain-mastery.proof: Solidity, Web3.js, OpenAI SDK — all shipped in production

founder-trajectory.title: Thinks Like an Owner — Because I'm Becoming One
founder-trajectory.description: Every project, certification, and skill has been deliberate preparation. The Gaia Project is my personal life OS. The Air Product is my first product built with founder proximity. SaaS in Southeast Asia is the destination.
founder-trajectory.proof: TeamStack → The Air Product → 3-Year SaaS Strategy

header.subtitle: Ordered by strategic signal strength: current flagship work, shipped AI, and the ownership stories behind each build

the-air-product.title: The Air Product
the-air-product.tagline: AI-first internal product with a 3-year strategic vision
the-air-product.problemSummary: The team lacked an intelligent, context-aware internal tool that adapted to real-world workflows.
the-air-product.problem: TeamStack is building an internal product that needs to think beyond simple dashboards. The tool must help the team perform better based on real context, support a long-term roadmap, and balance UX, product strategy, and technical feasibility from day one.
the-air-product.approach: I work closely with the CEO to translate the vision into a practical product architecture. I am responsible for the frontend experience, AI workflow design, and turning strategic requirements into a system that compounds value over time.
features: AI-assisted workflows designed from real usage context
Direct product direction alignment with the CEO
Architecture supporting a 3-year roadmap
Bridging product thinking, UX, and technical execution
the-air-product.outcomes: A strategic ongoing project reflecting deep product ownership, founder proximity, and practical AI product design.
the-air-product.challenges: The main challenge is designing for an evolving scope — translating strategic intent into a workable system without locking the team into rigid architecture.
the-air-product.whatIOwned: Architecture, frontend, AI workflow integration, and direct strategic collaboration with the CEO.

ai-event-platform.title: AI Event Creation Platform
ai-event-platform.tagline: Making event creation intelligent, not just digital
ai-event-platform.problemSummary: Event organizers spent too much time on repetitive content tasks that AI could reliably handle.
ai-event-platform.problem: Organizers have to write event descriptions, tweak copy, and make content decisions repeatedly under tight deadlines. The goal wasn't just an AI demo, but making AI a trusted part of a production workflow.
ai-event-platform.approach: I owned the OpenAI SDK integration into the live product, designed AI UX patterns for production, led the frontend delivery, and connected AI features to existing deployment and analytics systems.
features: Generative AI for automated event content creation
AI UX patterns designed for production reliability
Integration with analytics for post-launch measurement
Frontend experience that feels native to the product core
ai-event-platform.outcomes: Proven ability to bridge the gap between AI demos and AI products, creating patterns the team continues to use.
ai-event-platform.challenges: Balancing AI behavior with existing product expectations, managing prompt reliability, and ensuring UX confidence in a production environment.
ai-event-platform.whatIOwned: OpenAI SDK integration, AI UX patterns, full frontend delivery, and production rollout on AWS.

teamstack-roster.title: TeamStack Roster
teamstack-roster.tagline: Team management product shipped from 0 to MVP
teamstack-roster.problemSummary: High-performance teams need better tools for roster management and coordination.
teamstack-roster.problem: Managing teams and rosters in a real business context involves complex workflows beyond simple tables. It requires collaboration, handling frequent changes, and making product-level decisions on MVP scope.
teamstack-roster.approach: Owned the project from initial architecture to user-facing features, pushing the product from concept to a functional MVP by balancing system design, prioritization, and delivery quality.
features: Dynamic team and roster management workflows
MVP design focused on real-world utility
End-to-end ownership from architecture to release
Tight coordination with product stakeholders
teamstack-roster.outcomes: A clear evidence of 0→1 delivery and the first product I can point to as mine from system to experience.
teamstack-roster.challenges: Defining a sharp MVP scope without compromising the long-term product direction.
teamstack-roster.whatIOwned: Full product ownership: architecture, feature definition, and release readiness.

tangier-dao.title: Tangier — Decentralized Giving
tangier-dao.problemSummary: Communities need transparent, immutable systems for governance and fund allocation.
tangier-dao.problem: The goal was to build a governance system communities could trust. This wasn't just UI — it required smart contract logic for proposals, voting, and treasury behavior on-chain.
tangier-dao.approach: Developed the Solidity contracts, built the frontend, and connected Web3 flows to create a production-ready application.
features: Ethereum-based smart contract governance
Proposal and voting flows for community trust
Frontend bridging on-chain experience for users
System merging product and protocol thinking
tangier-dao.outcomes: A rare full-stack Web3 delivery proof in the local market, demonstrating frontier-tech capability.
tangier-dao.challenges: Designing for user trust and immutable contract behavior simultaneously, where mistakes are permanent.
tangier-dao.whatIOwned: Solidity contracts, full frontend delivery, and Web3 integration architecture.

token-gating.title: Token Gating — NFT Access Control
token-gating.tagline: Your NFT is your ticket, verified across chains
token-gating.problemSummary: Clients needed to control access based on NFT ownership without high user friction.
token-gating.problem: NFT access control is more than just checking tokens; it's about making the wallet flow, backend checks, and user experience smooth enough for production.
token-gating.approach: Created a reusable system connecting frontend, API, and Web3 checks to enable production-grade access control.
features: NFT ownership verification for access control
Wallet-integrated user flow
Full-stack verification: frontend to backend
Reusable integration pattern
token-gating.outcomes: Proven ability to learn new domains quickly and turn them into shippable production systems.
token-gating.whatIOwned: Full-stack delivery covering Vue 3 frontend, NestJS API, and multi-chain Web3 integration.

ap-thai.title: AP Thai — Property Discovery
ap-thai.tagline: Modernizing the home-buying experience for Thailand’s top developer
ap-thai.problemSummary: Property discovery needs to handle high scale and complex search behaviors on mobile-first devices.
ap-thai.problem: AP Thai needed a modern property discovery experience that worked at scale, including robust analytics and high-quality mobile usage.
ap-thai.approach: Led the core frontend development, including analytics instrumentation and test coverage for production stability.
features: Property discovery flow for high-scale real usage
Analytics instrumentation for data-driven decisions
Production-grade test coverage
